thesis: Packet is a mortal executable process body: it exists only while its process cost can be paid, and death is part of its semantics.
core_formula: life(packet) = ticks affordable by internal cost
death(packet) is semantic boundary, not implementation failure
bad trajectories must be allowed to die

internal_reading.zig_prototype: Shows the invariant physically: substrate, PU budget, chaos ticks, boundary crystallization, calm execution, tension, manifest, and halt.
internal_reading.mortality_myth: Shows the invariant mythically: body continuity is not packet identity, bad continuation can burn the current packet, and only compact death residue should pass forward.

layers.name: substrate
layers.meaning: hardware/runtime body: memory, IO, clock, PU budget
layers.invariant: a packet has an execution body and cannot live outside a sustaining substrate

layers.name: chaos
layers.meaning: living mutable field before stable form
layers.invariant: raw process life is self-modifying, irreversible, tick-bound, and not yet memory

layers.name: boundary
layers.meaning: observation, choice, and crystallization boundary between living pressure and formed execution
layers.invariant: form is selected and crystallized only through a costly boundary

layers.name: calm
layers.invariant: crystallized form can execute, but it is not identical to living chaos

layers.name: tension
layers.meaning: price of holding form against chaos and exhaustion
layers.invariant: a crystal must be held, reinforced, released back to chaos, or manifested

mortality.axiom: A packet is finite unless explicitly sustained.

death_modes.name: manifest_death
death_modes.meaning: local process ends by becoming output or boundary projection

death_modes.name: dissolve_death
death_modes.meaning: form loses sustain and returns to unresolved flow

death_modes.name: budget_death
death_modes.meaning: process loses the right to continue because existence cost is unpaid

death_modes.name: body_death
death_modes.meaning: embodied substrate can no longer host the current packet

death_modes.name: semantic_death
death_modes.meaning: identity of the packet does not continue, but compact residue may remain

mortality.residue_rule: Death may leave minimal process residue, not preserved identity.
mortality.death_residue_formula: this led to death; do not repeat it

economics.PU: process life capacity, not external accounting decoration
economics.loss: irreversible cost of forming, holding, transporting, or manifesting
economics.crystal_stock: encoded form may carry remaining life/cost as temporary stock
economics.continuation_rule: continuation requires budget, sustain, or successful reinforcement

operator_interpretation.packet_role: starts or continues raw process life
forms relation inside the body without making persistence by itself
loosens form, breaks stale continuation, or returns structure toward flow
crystallizes living pressure into a formed executable body
measures body/world/process state without becoming the body
extends form across ticks while cost remains payable
constrains continuation by law, likelihood, or admissibility
sustains, remembers, and spends the current process body
ends local process by exposing form to the world

packet_network_position.definition: packet-network is a packet body interpreted as receptive admission surface
packet_network_position.not_core_identity: packet is not reducible to network or transport
packet_network_position.core_relation: a mortal process body can receive operators, roles, contexts, and projections while it remains alive

implementation_relation.zig: raw invariant body; closest current source for packet mortality
implementation_relation.lua: likely research/runtime manifestation after law stabilizes

short_formula: body + budget + tick + topology = packet life
packet does not merely run; packet lives and can die
